source.book: 《9分达人雅思听力真题还原及解析6》

segments.text: Today's lecture is the first of a series of lectures on nanotechnology.
segments.translation: 今天这节课是纳米技术系列讲座的第一讲。

segments.text: Nanotechnology, sometimes shortened to 'nanotech', refers to the manipulation of matter on an atomic and molecular level.
segments.translation: 纳米技术，有时简称为'纳米科技'，指的是在原子和分子层面对物质进行操控。

segments.text: It's a science, engineering and technology conducted at the nanoscale, which is about 1 to 100 nanometres.
segments.translation: 它是一门在纳米尺度上开展的科学、工程和技术，纳米尺度大约是1到100纳米。

segments.text: It is the study and application of extremely small things and can be used across all the other science fields.
segments.translation: 它研究和应用极其微小的东西，可以运用到其他所有科学领域。

segments.text: Just how small is that?
segments.translation: 那到底有多小呢？

segments.text: A nanometre is one-billionth of a metre.
segments.translation: 一纳米是一米的十亿分之一。

segments.text: The idea of nanoscale research first started from a physicist called Richard Feynman in 1959.
segments.translation: 纳米尺度研究这个想法，最早是由一位名叫理查德·费曼的物理学家在1959年提出的。

segments.text: He envisioned a process in which scientists would be able to manipulate and control individual atoms and molecules.
segments.translation: 他设想了一个过程，让科学家能够操控和控制单个的原子和分子。

segments.text: In fact, it wasn't until 1981, with the invention of a special kind of microscope, that modern nanotechnology truly began.
segments.translation: 事实上，直到1981年一种特殊显微镜发明之后，现代纳米技术才真正起步。

segments.text: Even though nanotechnology has created many new materials and devices with a vast range of applications, there is always concern over these unnatural products.
segments.translation: 尽管纳米技术已经创造出许多应用范围极广的新材料和新设备，但人们始终对这些非天然的产品心存担忧。

segments.text: Many people are worried that they may pose a threat to the environment, health and safety, also known as EHS.
segments.translation: 很多人担心它们可能会对环境、健康和安全构成威胁，也就是所谓的EHS。

segments.text: In order to better understand and evaluate the potential harm, risk assessment has been introduced.
segments.translation: 为了更好地理解和评估潜在的危害，人们引入了风险评估。

segments.text: There are also ongoing international collaborations on nanoEHS research and related policy issues.
segments.translation: 在纳米EHS研究以及相关政策问题上，也有持续的国际合作。

segments.text: Despite the concern, nanotechnology has benefited society in unexpected ways.
segments.translation: 尽管存在这些担忧，纳米技术还是以意想不到的方式造福了社会。

segments.text: In the food processing industry, nanotechnology is leveraged against iron deficiency which triggers anaemia.
segments.translation: 在食品加工行业，纳米技术被用来对抗会引发贫血的缺铁问题。

segments.text: For example, by reducing the particle size in potatoes, this new technology improves the bioavailability of this nutritional ingredient without the tendency to cause colour and odour changes.
segments.translation: 比如说，通过缩小土豆中的颗粒尺寸，这项新技术提高了这种营养成分的生物利用率，而且不会导致颜色和气味发生变化。

segments.text: In terms of packaging, nanomaterials are more economical and are used in all sorts of products, chocolate in particular.
segments.translation: 在包装方面，纳米材料更加经济实惠，被用在各种各样的产品上，尤其是巧克力。

segments.text: Nanotechnology can also enhance the flavour of food.
segments.translation: 纳米技术还能提升食物的风味。

segments.text: On a domestic scale, we expect more 'smart' furniture, which is made of shape memory alloys, to be manufactured.
segments.translation: 在家居层面，我们预计会有更多用形状记忆合金制造的'智能'家具被生产出来。

segments.text: But nanotechnology has much wider application than this and could also help develop sustainable agriculture.
segments.translation: 但纳米技术的应用远不止于此，它还能帮助发展可持续农业。

segments.text: It offers great potential to strengthen the effect of fertilisers, thereby minimising the environmental impact and boosting the productivity of plants in the soil.
segments.translation: 它极有潜力增强肥料的效果，从而把对环境的影响降到最低，并提高土壤中植物的产量。

segments.text: In the area of medicine, bioengineers have developed simple and inexpensive nanoscale delivery vehicles.
segments.translation: 在医学领域，生物工程师已经研发出简单又便宜的纳米级递送载体。

segments.text: Conductors like carbon nanotubes have been invented with varying structures.
segments.translation: 像碳纳米管这样的导体已经被制造出来，结构多种多样。

segments.text: Such nanotubes have become increasingly attractive to researchers because of their ability to efficiently deliver drugs into veins.
segments.translation: 这类纳米管越来越受研究人员青睐，因为它们能高效地把药物送入静脉。

segments.text: This means that higher doses of drugs loaded on them can be transported from the administration sites to the effects-related sites, such as cancer focuses, thus decreasing toxic side effects while increasing the treatment effects.
segments.translation: 这意味着，装载在它们上面的更高剂量的药物，可以从给药部位输送到起效部位，比如癌症病灶，从而在增强治疗效果的同时减少毒副作用。

segments.text: The research team has also developed a widely accepted material that can be used to coat different surfaces, including wood, plastic and glass.
segments.translation: 研究团队还研发出一种被广泛认可的材料，可以用来涂覆各种表面，包括木头、塑料和玻璃。

segments.text: It is mainly comprised of silver.
segments.translation: 它主要由银构成。

segments.text: Such nanoparticles can eventually kill some microbes due to the antiseptic properties.
segments.translation: 这类纳米颗粒因为有杀菌特性，最终能够杀死一些微生物。

segments.text: The whole process is simple, easy to apply, and more importantly, harmless to the environment.
segments.translation: 整个过程简单、易于操作，更重要的是，对环境无害。

segments.text: Surprisingly, nanotechnology has major implications for our health as well.
segments.translation: 令人惊讶的是，纳米技术对我们的健康也有重大意义。

segments.text: By changing the metabolic rate through a particular type of protein, obese patients are able to burn calories faster even under a high-calorie diet.
segments.translation: 通过某种特定蛋白质来改变新陈代谢速率，肥胖患者即使在高热量饮食下也能更快地燃烧卡路里。

segments.text: Therefore, treatment programs are provided for patients to lose weight by disposing of excess fat in a relatively safer manner.
segments.translation: 因此，可以为患者提供治疗方案，以相对更安全的方式清除多余脂肪来减重。

segments.text: The finding may help explain why overweight people struggle to lose weight.
segments.translation: 这一发现或许能解释，为什么超重的人减肥这么困难。

segments.text: Their stored fat is actively fighting against their efforts to burn it off at the molecular level.
segments.translation: 他们储存的脂肪正在分子层面上，积极对抗他们想把它燃烧掉的努力。

segments.text: Further research is mainly focused on the clinical and commercial development of therapies for obesity, diabetes and other associated conditions such as heart disease.
segments.translation: 进一步的研究主要集中在针对肥胖、糖尿病以及心脏病等其他相关病症的疗法的临床和商业开发上。

segments.text: In terms of cosmetics, the applications of nanotechnology and nanomaterials can be found in many products.
segments.translation: 在化妆品方面，纳米技术和纳米材料的应用可以在许多产品中找到。

segments.text: There are two main uses for nanotechnology in cosmetics.
segments.translation: 纳米技术在化妆品中主要有两大用途。

segments.text: The first one is that nanoparticles are commonly used as UV filters in anti-aging suncare products to avoid skin damage caused by the sun.
segments.translation: 第一种是把纳米颗粒作为抗衰老防晒护理产品中的紫外线过滤剂，用来避免阳光对皮肤造成的伤害。

segments.text: In addition, nanotechnology has played an important role in delivering active ingredients to the skin.
segments.translation: 此外，纳米技术在把有效成分递送到皮肤方面也发挥了重要作用。

segments.text: The mechanism proves to improve the bioavailability of actives and enhance skin hydration.
segments.translation: 事实证明，这一机制能提高有效成分的生物利用率，增强皮肤的保湿效果。

segments.text: The wide variety of nanotechnology applications leave no doubt that this field will only continue to get more exciting, and I look forward to seeing these developments further branch out.
segments.translation: 纳米技术应用之广泛，让人毫不怀疑这一领域只会变得越来越精彩，我也很期待看到这些发展进一步开枝散叶。
